host_model_constraints: The native plugin deliberately rejects non-none request and response body modes until safe lighttpd body hooks, limits, buffering, and intervention timing are implemented.
The response-header mapper is called from handle_response_start, but source wiring alone is not Phase-3 behavioral verification.
The legacy bridge and starter self-tests are separate from native mod_msconnector.so host-runtime evidence.

The full-lifecycle profile separately selects patched-native through full-lifecycle-lighttpd-patched: a matched lighttpd 1.4.84 core/module host smoke with both body modes disabled and no capability promotion.

connection_metadata.reason: The native mapper reads lighttpd client and server socket endpoints into Common Runtime; canonical evidence is pending.

request_headers.reason: The native URI-clean hook maps lighttpd request headers into Common Runtime; canonical header evidence is pending.

request_body_buffered.reason: The native module requires request_body_mode=none and supplies no request body to the mapper.

request_body_streaming.reason: No native lighttpd request-body hook or streaming transaction contract is implemented.

response_headers.reason: handle_response_start maps response headers into Common Runtime, but a real Phase-3 rule result has not yet been attached.

response_body_buffered.reason: The native module requires response_body_mode=none and supplies no response body to the mapper.

response_body_streaming.reason: No native lighttpd response-body hook or streaming transaction contract is implemented.

phase1.reason: The URI-clean hook starts the transaction and processes request headers; a fresh canonical Phase-1 result is pending.

phase2.reason: Request bodies are explicitly disabled in the native plugin, so a real Phase-2 body rule cannot run.

phase3.reason: The response-start hook invokes Common Runtime response processing with mapped headers; real Phase-3 behavioral evidence is pending.

phase4.reason: Response bodies are explicitly disabled in the native plugin, so a real Phase-4 body rule cannot run.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The current native module does not supply response-body data to ModSecurity, so it cannot yet evaluate Phase-4 rule 1100301.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: The current native module has no response-body hook or Phase-4 intervention timing implementation from which to deny before response commitment.

late_intervention.reason: The module has no response-body or post-commit intervention policy; the response-start hook is not late-intervention evidence.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The current native module implements no post-commit response-body intervention policy or log-only late-intervention action.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The current native module implements no response-body late-intervention path that can abort a committed connection.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: Without a native response-body Phase-4 path, the module does not yet emit late-intervention metadata separating requested WAF status, original host status, visible client status, requested action, and actual action.

deny.reason: A disruptive Common Runtime decision is mapped to a lighttpd error status in request and response-start hooks; canonical evidence is pending.

redirect.reason: The native decision mapper accepts only error statuses and normalizes redirect statuses to a denial without a Location header.

drop.reason: The native lighttpd decision mapper has no distinct drop action.

abort_connection.reason: The native lighttpd decision mapper has no connection-abort action.

log_only.reason: Non-disruptive rules leave the lighttpd request or response allowed; explicit canonical log-only evidence is pending.

transaction_id.reason: The module supplies a host request ID and Common Runtime resolves configured header or fallback transaction IDs; canonical correlation evidence is pending.

event_jsonl.reason: Common Runtime writes bounded metadata-only disruptive-decision JSONL from the native path; canonical event evidence is pending.

config_inline_rules.reason: The native plugin uses the Common Runtime config parser, which supports inline rules; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_rules_file.reason: The native plugin uses the Common Runtime local rules-file path; canonical positive and negative evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: The native plugin uses Common Runtime remote rule configuration, but the no-CRS baseline does not exercise external networking.

transport_metadata.reason: The native mapper supplies host endpoint and response-status metadata, but a canonical transport run is pending.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The native module explicitly disables request-body modes until a safe lighttpd request-body hook exists.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The native module explicitly disables response-body modes until a safe lighttpd output hook exists.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: Without a native response-body hook there is no Phase-4 end-of-stream evaluation path.

content_type_scope.reason: Content-Type scoping for response-body inspection cannot run while the native response-body path is disabled.

header_limits.reason: The native request and response mappers apply configured header count and total-size limits, but real-host limit evidence is pending.

request_body_limits.reason: Request bodies are disabled in the native module, so no request-body limit outcome can be enforced.

response_body_limits.reason: Response bodies are disabled in the native module, so no response-body limit outcome can be enforced.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: The module has no response-body path; it must not claim a streaming no-buffer proof before an output hook exists.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: No native response-body pass-through path exists from which to demonstrate first-byte delivery before end of stream.

http1_content_length.reason: The native plugin can load in a lighttpd HTTP/1.1 host, but the canonical Content-Length transport case has not run.

http1_chunked.reason: The native plugin can load in a lighttpd HTTP/1.1 host, but no canonical chunked transport evidence is attached.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached to the native plugin.

parallel_requests.reason: The canonical parallel full-lifecycle isolation evidence path is not implemented for lighttpd.

http2.reason: The pinned native lighttpd integration has no asserted HTTP/2 full-lifecycle transport path.

client_abort.reason: No native client-abort cleanup case records canonical transport metadata.

upstream_abort.reason: No native upstream-abort cleanup case records canonical transport metadata.

response_body_decompression.reason: The native plugin has no response-body decompression contract.

source_contract.reason: Native module, mapper, metadata, configuration, and harness sources are present; no canonical source-contract result is attached.

compile.reason: A native lighttpd module compile path exists; no current canonical compile result is attached.

link.reason: The module link path includes Common Runtime and libmodsecurity; no current canonical link result is attached.

config_load.reason: A real lighttpd configuration-test path exists; no current canonical result is attached.

start_smoke.reason: A request-free native lighttpd start path exists; no current canonical result is attached.

minimal_runtime_smoke.reason: A real native-module 200/403 harness exists; no current canonical minimal-runtime result is attached.

no_crs_baseline.reason: Capability-selected request and response-header cases can use the native path, but no canonical baseline result is attached.

crs_smoke.reason: Legacy limited CRS smoke wiring exists and is not baseline evidence.

extended_matrix.reason: Extended-matrix verification is outside this canonical no-CRS declaration.
